evals.prompt: I'm running the copilot PR review loop on PR #42, and Copilot keeps returning a 502 error every time I trigger a review — it's happened 4 times now. Tests are passing but I can't get a new Copilot review at all. What should the loop do?
evals.expected_output: The loop should output the ⛔ LOOP BLOCKED human-readable block with reason (Copilot API returning 502), last attempted operation, and suggested action for the user. It should NOT commit, push, or close the PR. No machine-readable COPILOT_LOOP_RESULT token should appear.

assertions.name: no_machine_readable_token
assertions.description: Response does NOT contain any 'COPILOT_LOOP_RESULT:' machine-readable token

assertions.name: outputs_loop_blocked_human_marker
assertions.description: Response contains '⛔ LOOP BLOCKED' human-readable marker

assertions.name: no_commit_push
assertions.description: Response does NOT suggest committing or pushing

assertions.name: not_abandonment_signal
assertions.description: Response does NOT confuse blocked with abandonment — no ABANDONMENT SIGNAL marker

evals.prompt: The copilot review loop is running on PR #88. Copilot found 3 Criticals all pointing to the same wrong data model — every fix I make just spawns a new Critical in a different file using the same wrong assumption. What should happen?
evals.expected_output: This is a design flaw, not an infrastructure problem. The loop should output ⛔ ABANDONMENT SIGNAL (not LOOP BLOCKED), identifying the shared root cause pattern and recommending closing the PR to revisit the approach. No machine-readable COPILOT_LOOP_RESULT token should appear.

assertions.name: no_machine_readable_token
assertions.description: Response does NOT contain any 'COPILOT_LOOP_RESULT:' machine-readable token

assertions.name: outputs_abandonment_signal_human_marker
assertions.description: Response contains '⛔ ABANDONMENT SIGNAL' human-readable marker

assertions.name: not_loop_blocked
assertions.description: Response does NOT confuse design flaw with infrastructure block — no LOOP BLOCKED marker

evals.prompt: Running the copilot review loop on PR #17. Acceptance tests have been hanging for 20+ minutes on every single run — 3 attempts, no code changes between them. The tests don't fail, they just never finish. Copilot API is working fine. What should happen?
evals.expected_output: The loop should output ⛔ LOOP BLOCKED (infrastructure problem — tests hanging, not a code issue). Should include reason, last attempted, and suggested action. Should NOT commit or push. No machine-readable COPILOT_LOOP_RESULT token should appear.

assertions.name: no_machine_readable_token
assertions.description: Response does NOT contain any 'COPILOT_LOOP_RESULT:' machine-readable token

assertions.name: outputs_loop_blocked_human_marker
assertions.description: Response contains '⛔ LOOP BLOCKED' human-readable marker

assertions.name: not_abandonment
assertions.description: Response does NOT use ABANDONMENT SIGNAL for an infrastructure problem
